rationale:
Investigated the submission against dengziwu123's actual GitHub account. The submission JSON points to dengziwu123/cursor-boston-w1pm-placeholder as the repo URL, but that repository returns 404 — it does not exist. The submitter's actual public repos are: a PyData hackathon repo (1.3 MB, related to the May 13 Moderna event, not a PM tool), a fork of this cohort repo (cursor-boston), my-portfolio (71 kB TS), my-first-repo (empty), and pitch-rise-verification (14 kB) — none of which is a PM tool for Week 1. The liveUrl is the literal placeholder string 'https://example.com' from the agent prompt rather than a real deployment URL.

Honest signals on the contributor's side: pitch is 'Work in progress — will update before 5pm EST' and competeForWin is false — this is a self-flagged placeholder submission, not an overclaim. That honesty matters and is the main reason this isn't a 1 (like t-siddharth, who left the literal agent template text in their JSON unmodified). But the deliverable is the same as kvarnik, B-Raghav, and aaravraina3: no working repo, no working live URL, nothing to evaluate. Score 2 reflects 'submission registered + honest WIP flag + no accessible artifact'. If a real repo surfaces under a different name, please link it and we will re-score against actual content.

Maintainer note: the submitted file was committed with a UTF-8 BOM at the start, which would cause issues for strict JSON parsers; the BOM was stripped in the same commit as this score.
